christensen.mechanism: Piezoelectric transducers emit ultrasonic pulses through a fluid channel; the transit-time difference between upstream and downstream paths is proportional to fluid velocity
christensen.function: Measure volumetric flow rate and temperature of liquids with no moving parts and no pressure drop
christensen.outcome: Enable system controllers to optimize thermal energy transfer, detect faults, and reduce resource waste

technology.underlyingMechanism: Piezoelectric transducers (Piezo A / Piezo B) alternately transmit and receive ultrasonic pulses; the difference in transit time between upstream and downstream signal paths is proportional to fluid velocity; an embedded NTC thermistor provides simultaneous medium temperature
technology.unspscPath: 41 Laboratory and Measuring Equipment > 11 Measuring and Observing Instruments > 25 Liquid and Gas Flow Measuring > 01 Flowmeters

functionalPromise.statement: Measure volumetric flow rate and temperature of liquids with no moving parts and no pressure drop
functionalPromise.object: volumetric flow rate and temperature of liquids
functionalPromise.context: with no moving parts and no pressure drop

commodityFunctionalPromise: Quantify the volumetric or mass flow rate of fluids traversing a conduit
differentiators: No moving parts — unlike impeller/turbine sensors that suffer mechanical wear and particle jamming
Zero pressure drop — unlike orifice-plate, venturi, or vortex meters that obstruct flow
Simultaneous flow + temperature in a single sensor body — eliminates separate temperature probe
Tolerant of glycol mixtures and particulate suspensions — unlike turbine sensors degraded by particles
Bidirectional flow detection — inherent to transit-time principle, detects backflow without additional hardware

features.name: Transit-Time Flow Measurement
features.short: Measure fluid velocity via ultrasonic pulse transit-time difference
features.long: Two piezoelectric transducers alternately transmit and receive ultrasonic pulses. The difference in transit time between upstream and downstream paths is proportional to fluid velocity. Requires acoustic coupling between transducer and fluid. Accuracy degrades with air bubble content >2% by volume.

features.name: Integrated Temperature Sensing
features.short: Measure medium temperature via embedded NTC thermistor
features.long: NTC element integrated into the wetted flow body provides continuous medium temperature reading alongside flow measurement. Eliminates need for separate temperature probe. Enables real-time temperature compensation of speed-of-sound calculations.

features.name: Zero Pressure Drop Flow Path
features.short: Pass fluid through an unobstructed bore with no flow restriction
features.long: Straight-through flow channel has no impeller, vortex-shedding body, or orifice plate. Fluid energy is not consumed by the measurement. Eliminates head-loss penalty in system hydraulic design.

features.name: No Moving Parts
features.short: Operate without any mechanical components in the flow path
features.long: Entirely solid-state measurement principle. No impeller bearings, no rotating shafts, no seal wear. Eliminates mechanical failure modes and the associated maintenance schedule. Service life limited by electronics and housing seals, not by mechanical wear.

features.name: Bidirectional Flow Detection
features.short: Detect and measure both forward and reverse flow
features.long: Transit-time principle inherently distinguishes flow direction. The sign of the transit-time difference inverts with flow reversal. Enables backflow detection and alarm without additional check valves or sensors.

features.name: Digital Anomaly Detection
features.short: Detect bubbles, empty pipes, and glycol presence in the medium
features.long: Onboard signal processing identifies characteristic acoustic signatures of air bubbles, pipe-empty conditions, and glycol concentration changes. Provides diagnostic flags to the host controller for system health monitoring.

features.name: Real-Time Compensation Algorithms
features.short: Compensate flow readings for temperature, pressure, and viscosity variations
features.long: Firmware algorithms adjust transit-time calculations based on measured temperature and known fluid properties. Reduces systematic measurement error across the operating envelope. Requires fluid type to be configured at commissioning.

features.name: Modular Pipe Diameter Variants
features.short: Serve multiple pipe sizes via interchangeable flow bodies
features.long: DN12, DN14, DN20, DN25 flow body variants share the same electronics platform and RAST 2.5 connector. Customer selects flow body matching their pipe diameter. DN20 is serial production; DN12, DN14, DN25 are concept/development.

features.name: Customizable Communication Interface
features.short: Support UART, LIN, and frequency output protocols
features.long: Electronics can be configured for UART digital bus, LIN automotive-grade bus, or frequency pulse output. Selectable at the order level. Enables integration into diverse control architectures without hardware changes.

features.name: Customizable Mechanical Interface
features.short: Accept threaded or plug-in pipe connections
features.long: Flow body inlet/outlet can be threaded (standard plumbing) or plug-in (quick-connect for OEM assembly lines). Reduces OEM installation time and allows adaptation to different piping standards.

features.name: Low-Energy Battery Mode
features.short: Operate at ultra-low current consumption for battery-powered applications
features.long: Low-energy firmware variant reduces standby current to <5 uA, enabling battery-powered remote installations without mains power. Trades measurement frequency for power savings.

features.name: Pre-Calibrated Delivery
features.short: Ship fully calibrated and tested to customer-specific medium and flow profile
features.long: Each sensor is individually calibrated against the customer's specified medium and tested across the operating flow profile before delivery. Includes 100% automated final test. Eliminates field calibration requirement.

features.name: KTW/WRAS Drinking Water Approval
features.short: Wetted parts approved for potable water contact
features.long: Housing materials and seals meet KTW (German) and WRAS (UK) drinking water contact standards. Enables deployment in potable water circuits without additional material qualification.

constraints.name: Media Limitation
constraints.description: Only water and water-like media (incl. glycol, particulate suspensions). Cannot measure oils, gases, or non-polar liquids due to acoustic impedance mismatch.

constraints.name: Maximum Medium Temperature
constraints.description: Polymer housing and seals degrade above 90C. Extended operation near limit reduces service life.

constraints.name: Maximum Operating Pressure
constraints.description: Housing structural integrity rated to 10 bar continuous. Burst pressure 9 or 42 bar depending on variant.

constraints.name: Minimum Flow Rate Detection
constraints.description: Signal-to-noise ratio degrades at very low flow rates. Transit-time difference becomes indistinguishable from noise. Lower bound varies by pipe diameter.

constraints.name: Air Bubble Tolerance
constraints.description: Air entrainment >2% by volume attenuates ultrasonic signal and introduces measurement error. Systems with chronic air entrainment require de-aerators upstream.

constraints.name: Pipe Full Condition
constraints.description: Pipe must be fully filled with liquid for valid measurement. Partially filled pipes cause complete signal loss.

constraints.name: Minimum Straight Pipe Run
constraints.description: Turbulent or asymmetric flow profiles from upstream disturbances degrade accuracy. Recommended straight pipe run required upstream.

constraints.name: Temperature-Dependent Accuracy
constraints.description: Speed of sound in the medium changes with temperature. Without compensation, error reaches ~1.5%. Built-in NTC compensation reduces to ~0.2%, but extreme transients may temporarily exceed compensation response.

billOfMaterials.function: Emit and receive ultrasonic pulses for transit-time flow measurement
Simultaneous medium temperature measurement alongside flow

Calculate transit-time difference, apply compensation algorithms, generate output signal

billOfMaterials.function: Electrical interface to host controller (UART, LIN, or frequency output)
billOfMaterials.keyAttribute: Configurable at order level: UART / LIN / frequency pulse

billOfMaterials.function: Hydraulic sealing of the flow body at pressure rating
Couple piezoelectric transducers to the fluid channel wall
billOfMaterials.keyAttribute: Matched acoustic impedance to water/glycol media
